title: Emit a first-class doc-evidence-missing audit finding (computed but never reported)
description:
`shared/inventory.py:530` computes `docs_missing` (declared Evidence.Docs paths that do not resolve), but `shared/audit.py` never emits a finding for it — broken doc evidence only surfaces indirectly via claim-unsupported. Empirical impact (bento corpus): a story whose subject skill was renamed declares doc evidence at the dead path `catalog/skills/issue-completeness-precheck/SKILL.md`; the audit produced no direct finding for the dangling path. "Story references a removed/renamed file" is precisely the drift class story audits should catch first — it is mechanical, zero-judgment, and high-signal.

Work: add a `doc-evidence-missing` finding kind to audit.py (per story, per unresolved path), include it in strict-mode failure, document it in the SKILL.md finding table, and add a test with a story declaring a nonexistent doc path.

Acceptance: an audit over a corpus with one dangling doc path reports exactly one doc-evidence-missing finding naming story and path; strict mode fails.

Note: dedupe against claim-unsupported — when one root cause would trigger both findings for the same story, either suppress the indirect finding or cross-reference it; pick one behavior and test it.

title: Stories are never committed and audits never scheduled: add a commit step and a landing-workflow integration
description:
The workflow has no persistence or enforcement loop: (1) stories-init's Finish section (`skills/stories-init/SKILL.md:171-175`) says review/rebuild/verify/report — no commit; `grep -i 'commit\|push'` across all six SKILL.md files and the 484-line spec.md finds nothing relevant. Observed result in bento: 22 generated stories sat untracked for weeks, invisible to git and to stories-impact-check. (2) stories-init's gitignore step (drift-todo entry) half-applied in bento and nothing re-verified it. (3) stories-audit is read-only and nothing ever schedules it — no landing-workflow integration exists, so drift detection never actually runs.

Work: (a) add an explicit Commit step to the init/generate/update Finish sections ("stories are durable repo artifacts; commit docs/stories/ and the .gitignore change on the feature branch"); (b) ship a land-work integration: an installable hook-script for the bento land-work extension convention (`<root>/land-work/hook-scripts/pre/NN-stories-audit.sh`) that runs the strict audit when `docs/stories/INDEX.md` exists, so audits gate landings in repos using both plugins; (c) have stories-init verify at Finish that the gitignore change actually landed.

Acceptance: a fresh stories-init run ends with committed stories and a verified gitignore entry; in a repo with the hook installed, a landing with failing strict audit is blocked.

Post-review descope: sub-item (b) — the land-work hook integration — is split into its own issue (land-work-audit-hook). This issue now covers only (a) the Commit step in the init/generate/update Finish sections and (c) stories-init verifying at Finish that the gitignore change actually landed. Acceptance narrows accordingly: a fresh stories-init run ends with committed stories and a verified gitignore entry.

Clarifications (second review pass): the gitignore verification belongs to stories-init only. The Commit-step wording should say "commit on the current branch" and note that branch/worktree conventions belong to the host repo's workflow, out of scope here. Acceptance verification: a scripted or manual fresh-init run on a fixture repo; automated test optional.
close_reason: Landed via merge 3a130aa: added Commit step to stories-init/generate/update Finish sections, plus stories-init Finish gitignore-landed verification. Generated mirrors rebuilt; full test suite passes.

title: SKILL.md script paths do not resolve in the Claude install layout; no plugin-root discovery preamble
description:
In the Claude layout, skills ship only SKILL.md (+packaging.json) and all scripts live at the plugin root (`shared/`, `scripts/`). But `skills/stories-update/SKILL.md:36,162` instructs `stories-audit/scripts/audit.py --repo-root ...` and `skills/stories-impact-check/SKILL.md:42` instructs `stories-impact-check/scripts/impact_check.py ...` — those per-skill `scripts/` dirs exist only in the `.codex-plugin` layout. Actual files: `shared/audit.py`, `shared/impact_check.py`. No storystore SKILL.md explains how to locate the plugin root (contrast bugshot's "find the directory containing this SKILL.md" pattern).

Work: standardize all SKILL.md invocations on `<storystore plugin root>/shared/<script>.py` with a one-line plugin-root discovery preamble per skill; verify against both install layouts.

Acceptance: every documented invocation resolves from a consumer repo in the Claude cache layout; same for Codex layout.

Verification method: build/install both layouts (the Claude cache layout and the .codex-plugin layout) into temp dirs and resolve every documented invocation path from a temp consumer repo; automate as a test if cheap.

title: stories-audit and stories-coverage SKILL.md claim "Implementation deferred to Plan 2" while the implementation ships, tested
description:
`skills/stories-audit/SKILL.md:24-25` and `skills/stories-coverage/SKILL.md:20-21` say "**Status:** Implementation deferred to Plan 2." But `shared/audit.py` is 630 lines and `shared/coverage.py` 705 lines, both with test suites (`tests/test_storystore_audit.py`, `tests/test_storystore_coverage.py`). The SKILL.md is the only thing the agent reads: any agent invoking `storystore:stories-audit` concludes the capability does not exist and stops. This neuters the plugin's core value (drift detection) — observed in practice in the bento repo, where the generated story corpus has never been audited.

Work: rewrite both SKILL.md files with the actual invocation (script path, flags like `--repo-root`, `--story <slug>`, `--strict`), output format, and exit codes; add a release check that greps SKILL.md files for "deferred"/"not implemented" before publishing.

Acceptance: an agent following either SKILL.md verbatim successfully runs the audit/coverage pass; release check in place.

Release-check placement: locate the existing publish/verification pipeline and add the "deferred"-grep there; if no such pipeline exists, add it as a repo test (the cheapest enforceable home).
notes:
Pickpackit field evidence (filed 2026-06-12; from issue-draft batch /home/ketan/tmp/pickpackit-issue-drafts/upstream/storystore/01-audit-coverage-field-evidence.md):

The installed plugin cache at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/bento/storystore/0.1.1/ is stale: its skills/stories-audit/SKILL.md (line 24) and skills/stories-coverage/SKILL.md (line 20) still say "**Status:** Implementation deferred to Plan 2", even though the implementations ship in the repo. Real-world consequence observed in /home/ketan/project/pickpackit:

- 5 stories under docs/stories/ were created 2026-05-20 by init-seeding.
- last_audited was never set on any of them (no story file contains the field with a value).
- Over the following 3 weeks, every story accumulated broken or fabricated evidence undetected: two wholly fabricated API endpoints (GET /api/workspaces/{workspace_id}/bootstrap in docs/stories/dashboard-trip-overview.md:41 — the real endpoint is /overview; DELETE /trips/{trip_id}/packing-items/{packing_item_id} in docs/stories/trip-packing-list-management.md:35,46 — removal is actually POST .../remove), plus sed-corrupted file paths like web/src/pages/My stuff.test.tsx and route /my stuff in docs/stories/my-stuff-management.md:41,47.
- The cached stub skills, when triggered, printed their deferred-status text and exited cleanly, so no agent ever learned that an audit was supposed to happen but could not. The silence is what let 3 weeks of drift accumulate.

This evidence prioritizes the fix against demonstrated real-world cost, not just doc hygiene. Companion issue filed for the stub failure mode: ss-c2v (stub/deferred skills must fail loudly when invoked instead of exiting clean).
close_reason: Rewrote skills/stories-audit/SKILL.md and skills/stories-coverage/SKILL.md with real invocation (script paths, flags, JSON output, exit codes), verified against shared/audit.py and shared/coverage.py. Added stub-language release gate (TestNoStubLanguageInSkills) in tests/test_storystore_plugin_contracts.py that fails publish on 'deferred'/'not implemented' in any SKILL.md, plus a scan-coverage guard. Full suite 584 passed. Landed via --no-ff merge 6773805.

title: Fix shared spec materialization drift
description: Resolve the build-plugin collision where skills/stories-audit/references/spec.md differs from shared/spec.md even though stories-audit declares shared/spec.md as a shared materialized file. This blocks scripts/build-plugin and the build-plugin pytest suite, including landing unrelated skill updates. Keep the change scoped to shared-file materialization drift; do not change stories-init guidance in this task.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/build-plugin -v completes without shared-file collision; tests/test_build_plugin.py and tests/test_build_plugin_shared_scripts.py pass; changed files are limited to the shared materialized copies required to make build-plugin deterministic.
notes: No tracked repository change was needed: current local main builds cleanly. The collision was stale ignored materialized files in the codex/stories-init-conventions worktree. Removed regenerated ignored shared directories there, reran scripts/build-plugin -v successfully, and focused tests now pass (33 passed).
close_reason: No tracked repo change required; stale ignored materialized files were refreshed and 5eb465b87b2b71870bf1ce7767d55db8e7d126e3 landed on main after verification

title: Update stories-init skill for project work conventions
description: Update the stories-init skill so agents initializing docs/stories/ follow repository work-organization conventions before creating or modifying files. The skill should cover branch and linked-worktree expectations, tracker discovery/claiming, local instruction files, documentation placement, verification, and generated plugin output handling where applicable. Keep the update scoped to stories-init workflow guidance, not implementation of the deferred runtime scripts.
acceptance_criteria: stories-init/SKILL.md documents how to discover and follow repo conventions for worktrees, branches, issue trackers, local instructions, docs, and verification; guidance is concise and consistent with existing storystore source docs; generated Claude/Codex plugin payloads are refreshed if build output changes; relevant validation/build tests pass.
notes: Updated stories-init guidance on branch codex/stories-init-conventions (commit 9ccff8a) with a shorter convention checklist. Validation: quick_validate passed. Build-plugin tests currently fail on existing shared spec collision: skills/stories-audit/references/spec.md differs from shared/spec.md.

title: Add mutation and regression tests for policy gates
description: Add systematic mutation tests for change-resistance and lock policy gates.
acceptance_criteria: Generate controlled mutations to frontmatter, locked sections, inline locked blocks, auditable claims, evidence, and metadata; assert edit_section.py allows/refuses each mutation according to change resistance; cover low, medium, high, and immutable; include negative tests for forbidden immutable changes even with confirmation flags.

title: Build fixture-driven functional test harness
description: Create reusable fixture repositories and helpers for functional script tests beyond smoke coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Add reusable fixture repos for empty, TypeScript CLI, HTTP API, docs-heavy, malformed-story, and drift scenarios; provide helpers to copy fixtures into temp dirs and run skill scripts against them; tests assert exact JSON shapes and meaningful Markdown report fragments; fixtures are small, explicit, and committed.

title: Implement stories-impact-check skill
description: Add the read-only stories-impact-check skill workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Add stories-impact-check/SKILL.md; hard-trigger description is direct and load-bearing; documents resistance behavior table; skill is read-only; build includes skill in Claude and Codex outputs.

title: Implement impact check runtime
description: Implement impact_check.py for matching planned behavior changes to stories.
acceptance_criteria: Add impact_check.py; match by file evidence, surface evidence, source-defining surface, and description tokens; support repeatable --file, repeatable --surface, single --description; OR-combine dimensions; emit match metadata and perf block; tests cover matching, repeated description refusal, stale deprecated matches, and observed-authority non-gating data.

title: Implement stories-update skill orchestration
description: Add the stories-update skill orchestration flow around scoped audit, drift triage, and guarded edits.
acceptance_criteria: Add stories-update/SKILL.md; requires scoped stories-audit --story <slug> before edits; documents drift classification flow; routes code-side bugs to drift_todo.py; states edit classification before applying story-side edits; cross-references stories-impact-check for behavior changes; build includes skill in Claude and Codex outputs.

title: Implement story lock and section edit primitives
description: Implement lock_check.py and edit_section.py policy primitives for guarded story edits.
acceptance_criteria: Add lock_check.py; add edit_section.py; enforce locked sections, inline locked blocks, auditable claim count guard, immutable restrictions, and resistance-change confirmation; regenerate INDEX.md on metadata-affecting changes; do not bump last_audited; tests cover policy refusals and allowed edits.

title: Implement stories-coverage
description: Implement software-to-story coverage reporting and completeness scoring.
acceptance_criteria: Add stories-coverage skill and coverage.py; emit surface-uncovered, story-untested, and story-incomplete; implement completeness scoring and rating thresholds; include Language Coverage and placeholder Intent header notes; support --strict, --thorough, source scoping, limits, and perf output; tests cover findings, scoring, modes, and exit codes.

title: Implement stories-audit
description: Implement read-only fidelity audit reporting.
acceptance_criteria: Add stories-audit skill and audit.py; emit Markdown report and stdout JSON; support full audit, --story, --bump-clean, --thorough, --strict, and perf threshold flags/env; emit deterministic findings and agent-pointer-missing; include Language Coverage header; tests cover findings, modes, exit codes, and perf output.

title: Implement inventory and evidence resolution
description: Implement user-facing surface inventory, language detection, and evidence resolution.
acceptance_criteria: Add inventory.py; detect languages by marker files at depth <= 2; extract TypeScript/JavaScript user-facing surfaces; resolve test, surface, and docs evidence refs; skip vendored/build/cache dirs; support source-root and include-dir behavior in library APIs; tests cover extraction, language detection, and skipped directories.
close_reason: Implemented in shared/inventory.py with test_storystore_inventory.py covering language detection, surface extraction, and evidence resolution. Landed via merge commit on main.

title: Implement storystore_lib frontmatter and story parsing
description: Implement strict stdlib story parsing and validation.
acceptance_criteria: Add strict stdlib frontmatter parser; validate allowed fields, enums, list syntax, unknown keys, and line/column errors; enforce observed/high matrix and tests_applicable conflict; parse H2 sections, locked blocks, evidence subsections, and story files; tests cover parser and loader behavior.

title: Add shared-script packaging support
description: Teach the build to materialize shared storystore files into canonical and generated skill payloads.
acceptance_criteria: Build reads packaging.json; supports shared_scripts; materializes shared files into canonical skill dirs and generated plugin outputs; supports --shared-only; rejects malformed packaging, missing files, escaping paths, non-string entries, and collisions; preserves executable mode; tests cover packaging behavior.

title: Author stories-init and stories-generate skill workflows
description: Add agent-facing workflow docs for stories-init and stories-generate.
acceptance_criteria: Add stories-init/SKILL.md; add stories-generate/SKILL.md; stories-init documents Phase 2 LLM flow for fresh init only; stories-generate documents interview and observed modes; skills reference script contracts accurately; build includes both skills in Claude and Codex outputs.

title: Implement candidate discovery for observed stories
description: Implement list_candidates.py for observed-mode story candidate discovery.
acceptance_criteria: Add list_candidates.py; detect TypeScript CLI commands, HTTP routes, package bin, package exports, tests, README/DESIGN headings, and useful scripts; subtract already-authored story slugs; return stable JSON; fixture tests cover each candidate source.

title: Implement story schema writer and index generation
description: Implement write_story.py for schema-compliant story creation and INDEX regeneration.
acceptance_criteria: Add write_story.py; support --interview and --observed defaults; enforce slug rules, validity matrix, and overwrite refusal; write schema-compliant story markdown; regenerate docs/stories/INDEX.md slug-sorted; tests cover validation and index cases.

title: Implement stories-init mechanical setup
description: Implement the mechanical Phase 1 setup script for stories-init.
acceptance_criteria: Create docs/stories/ when missing; write missing README.md stub and empty INDEX.md; add docs/stories/drift-todo.md to .gitignore; detect root AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md; return JSON with fresh_init, created, preserved, gitignore_updated, and agent_instruction_files; tests cover idempotency and preservation.

title: Bootstrap storystore as a standalone Claude/Codex plugin repo
description: Set up the repo layout and build workflow for a single storystore plugin that emits both Claude and Codex plugin payloads. Use Bugshot's standalone shape as the closest base, with Bento's plugin metadata conventions where useful.
acceptance_criteria: Repo has .claude-plugin/plugin.json and .codex-plugin/plugin.json; repo has canonical source directories for skills, scripts, assets, tests, and generated plugin outputs; scripts/build-plugin generates both Claude and Codex payloads; plugin metadata matches the target design: name storystore, display name Storystore, six stories-* skills; basic smoke build passes.

title: Redirect runtime-file references away from export-ignored plan docs to spec.md
description:
After ss-15i excluded the internal plan docs from the published plugin bundle
(via `.gitattributes export-ignore` on `2026-05-01-storystore-plan-*.md` and
`2026-05-01-storystore-target-design.md`), six shipped runtime files still
point readers to those now-excluded docs as "the full contract" / "original
design doc". In a consumer's install the referenced files do not exist.

The authoritative contract `spec.md` still ships and is already cited alongside
each broken pointer, so nothing is functionally broken — this is doc-quality
cleanup. Redirect each plan-doc pointer to `spec.md` (or drop the dangling
reference where `spec.md` is already named).

Affected files and lines (verified at ss-15i landing):
- skills/stories-audit/SKILL.md:25        -> plan-2-fidelity.md
- skills/stories-coverage/SKILL.md:21      -> plan-2-fidelity.md
- skills/stories-impact-check/SKILL.md:145 -> plan-3-edits-and-impact.md
- skills/stories-update/SKILL.md:251       -> plan-3-edits-and-impact.md
- shared/audit.py:8                        -> plan-2-fidelity.md (docstring)
- shared/impact_check.py:7                 -> plan-3-edits-and-impact.md (docstring)

Routing notes:
- stories-audit and stories-coverage SKILL.md are already being rewritten under
  ss-jzs; fold those two redirects into that work.
- stories-impact-check and stories-update SKILL.md need separate attention here.
- shared/audit.py and shared/impact_check.py are one-line docstring changes each.
- Remember SKILL.md edits must go through the canonical skills/<name>/SKILL.md
  source and a `scripts/build-plugin` rebuild (the `.claude/` and
  `.codex-plugin/` copies are build outputs).

Acceptance: no shipped runtime file references an export-ignored plan/design
doc; each former pointer resolves to spec.md or is removed.
acceptance_criteria: No shipped runtime file references an export-ignored plan/design doc; each former pointer resolves to spec.md or is removed.

title: stories-generate/stories-init must deterministically verify evidence refs before writing, with a bounded unverified fallback
description:
## Symptom / motivation (pickpackit case study)

stories-init seeded 5 observed-mode stories in the pickpackit repo
(`/home/ketan/project/pickpackit/docs/stories/`) on 2026-05-20. The evidence
sections were written from the model's memory of the codebase, not from the
route table, and contained:

- Two wholly fabricated endpoints:
  - `GET /api/workspaces/{workspace_id}/bootstrap` in
    `docs/stories/dashboard-trip-overview.md:41` — the real endpoint is
    `/api/workspaces/{workspace_id}/overview`.
  - `DELETE /trips/{trip_id}/packing-items/{packing_item_id}` in
    `docs/stories/trip-packing-list-management.md:35,46` — removal is
    actually `POST .../packing-items/{packing_item_id}/remove`.
- 4 of 5 stories omitted the `/api` mount prefix on their route evidence
  (e.g. `PATCH /trips/{trip_id}/packing-items/{packing_item_id}` in
  `docs/stories/quick-pack-departure.md:34,43`), so even the "real" refs do
  not match what the server serves.

The "do not invent" rule already exists in prose — verified anchors:
`skills/stories-generate/SKILL.md:40` ("Do not invent failure modes the user
has not accepted"), the observed-mode drafting step around line 71 ("Capture
only failure behavior that the evidence supports — do not fabricate"), and
`shared/spec.md:138` ("Do not invent edge cases or failure..."). Nothing
enforces it: generation writes whatever the model produces, and the
fabrications shipped and sat undetected for 3 weeks (the audit skill that
would have caught them was a stub in the installed 0.1.1 cache — see
ss-jzs).

## Desired behavior / change

stories-generate and stories-init must deterministically verify evidence
refs before writing a story to disk, with a bounded fallback (pinned by the
user):

- Deterministic resolution is REQUIRED only for evidence kinds that are
  mechanically checkable: file paths, test paths, and routes extractable
  from typed sources (e.g. an axum/router route table, an OpenAPI document).
- For any ref that cannot be deterministically resolved, mark the ref
  `unverified` in the written story rather than blocking generation. Do not
  silently write it as if it were verified.
- A ref that IS mechanically checkable and FAILS resolution (file does not
  exist, route not in the table) must block the write or be dropped with an
  explicit report — never written as clean evidence.

## Acceptance checks

- Generating a story whose evidence cites a nonexistent file path or a
  route absent from the project's extractable route surface either fails
  the draft gate or writes the ref explicitly marked `unverified`/failed —
  it never appears as ordinary evidence.
- A fixture reproducing the pickpackit pattern (fabricated endpoint +
  missing mount prefix) demonstrates the new behavior in tests.
- Refs that are genuinely outside deterministic reach (prose claims, UI
  behavior) still generate, marked `unverified`, so generation is not
  blocked on unresolvable kinds.

## In scope / out of scope

- In scope: verification at generate/init time; the `unverified` marking;
  reusing the existing inventory/evidence-resolution machinery
  (`shared/spec.md` inventory sections) at write time.
- Out of scope: rubric/scoring changes (ss-8sn); retroactively fixing
  the pickpackit stories; audit-time detection (already specced
  separately).

title: Stub/deferred skills must fail loudly when invoked instead of exiting clean
description:
## Symptom / motivation (pickpackit case study)

A skill whose SKILL.md declares itself deferred/stubbed gives the invoking
agent a clean exit and no signal that the capability it asked for does not
exist in the installed version. Field case: the installed plugin cache at
`~/.claude/plugins/cache/bento/storystore/0.1.1/` ships stories-audit and
stories-coverage SKILL.md files still marked "**Status:** Implementation
deferred to Plan 2" (the outdated text itself is tracked by ss-jzs). In
`/home/ketan/project/pickpackit`, the cached stub skills, when triggered,
printed their deferred-status text and exited cleanly, so no agent ever
learned that an audit was supposed to happen but could not. The silence let
3 weeks of evidence drift accumulate across 5 init-seeded stories
(fabricated endpoints, sed-corrupted paths — full field evidence attached to
ss-jzs as a note).

## Desired behavior / change

Any skill shipped in a stub/deferred state must FAIL LOUDLY when invoked —
emit an actionable error that names the shipped plugin version and states
that the implementation is not present in this version (e.g. "stories-audit
is a stub in storystore 0.1.1; upgrade the plugin or run from a source
checkout"), rather than printing status prose and exiting clean. The
invoking agent must be able to distinguish "audit ran, no findings" from
"audit did not run."

## Acceptance checks

- Any remaining or future stub SKILL.md instructs the agent to surface an
  error naming the shipped version instead of exiting clean.
- Invoking a stub skill in a test or fixture repo produces a visibly
  non-successful outcome distinguishable from a clean no-findings run.

## In scope / out of scope

- In scope: defining and enforcing the loud-failure contract for
  stub/deferred skills.
- Out of scope: refreshing the user's installed plugin cache (deployment
  concern); rewriting the outdated SKILL.md text itself (ss-jzs);
  generation-time evidence verification (ss-xdk) and rubric accuracy
  (ss-8sn).

title: Ship a copyable landing-gate hook script that runs the strict stories audit before merges
description:
stories-audit is read-only and nothing ever schedules it, so drift detection never actually runs in consuming repos (observed: a 22-story corpus generated 2026-05-24 in a consumer repo was never audited once). The bento land-work skill has a documented project extension point: executable hook scripts at <repo-root>/land-work/hook-scripts/pre/NN-slug.sh run before any merge and block on nonzero exit.

Work: provide a copyable, tested example hook script (e.g. examples/land-work/hook-scripts/pre/30-stories-audit.sh) that: exits 0 immediately when docs/stories/INDEX.md is absent; otherwise runs the strict audit (shared/audit.py --repo-root <root> --strict, path resolved via the plugin-root preamble from the script-paths issue) and exits nonzero on findings. Document it in stories-init's Finish section ("install the landing gate"). Delivery mechanism beyond a documented copyable example (e.g. automated install) is a follow-up decision delegated to the implementer; the minimum acceptable outcome is the documented, tested example.

Acceptance: the example script exists, is referenced from stories-init, and in a repo with it installed a landing with a failing strict audit is blocked while a clean audit passes through.

Clarifications (second review pass): depends on script-paths-claude-layout (the plugin-root preamble) — declare the dependency in the tracker. Tests: a repo test that runs the example script against a fixture repo (one passing corpus, one with an injected failing finding) asserting exit codes. Coordinate with the publish-bundle-contents issue so examples/ is retained in the published bundle.

title: Gate stories-impact-check's hard trigger on stories existing; document the observed-to-accepted promotion path
description:
`skills/stories-impact-check/SKILL.md:3-9` declares "Hard trigger — before any behavioral change to user-facing surfaces, run this skill" with no existence gate — it fires in every repo, including those with no docs/stories/. And per its own behavior table (~lines 126-133), `observed`-authority stories are "Informational only. Non-gating regardless of resistance" — in a freshly seeded corpus (all stories observed, as in bento) the hard trigger taxes every edit while being unable to gate anything. Agents will learn to ignore it — the worst outcome for a hard trigger.

Work: add an existence gate to the description ("when docs/stories/INDEX.md exists"); document the promotion path (observed → accepted via human review) prominently in stories-update or stories-init so corpora do not remain permanently non-gating; consider an init-time note telling the user gating is inert until stories are accepted.

Acceptance: the trigger fires only in story-bearing repos; the promotion path is documented where a user setting up a corpus will see it.

Acceptance (amended): the published SKILL.md description text contains the existence gate (docs/stories/INDEX.md present), verified by reading the file; the promotion-path documentation is present in stories-init or stories-update where a user seeding a corpus will see it.
close_reason: Landed on main (merge 80461e4). Added docs/stories/INDEX.md existence gate to stories-impact-check description + body; documented observed->accepted + draft->active promotion path in stories-init. Published .claude/ and .codex-plugin/ copies rebuilt. 584 tests pass.

title: Support skill/markdown repos: a skill: surface prefix or doc-directory extractor (current extractors are TS/JS-only)
description:
Storystore's surface extractors cover TypeScript/JavaScript only (`shared/inventory.py:60`); in a Python+markdown repo (e.g. bento, a skills marketplace) the audit reports "Detected: (none), Extracted: (none)" and every surface ref is unmatched. Worse, the corpus storystore itself generated in bento uses a `skill:<name>` surface prefix that the validator rejects ("could not be parsed" — `skill:` is not in spec.md's prefix set of cli:/route:/bin:/exports:/test:/heading:/schema:). Empirical result: 25 of 26 audit findings on the bento corpus were structural noise from unparseable/unmatchable surface refs, drowning the one real finding.

Work: either add a `skill:` prefix to the spec with an extractor that inventories skill directories (e.g. `catalog/skills/<name>/` or installed-skill names), or have generation emit only spec-valid refs (heading:/doc-based) in repos without supported languages; fix the generator so it cannot emit refs its own validator rejects; add a generation-then-audit round-trip test on a markdown-only fixture repo.

Acceptance: generation output validates clean against the spec; an audit on a markdown-only repo produces zero structural-noise findings.

Unresolved design decision (triage): choose between (i) adding a skill: prefix to the spec plus a skill-directory inventory extractor, or (ii) constraining generation to emit only spec-valid refs in unsupported-language repos. The invariant that generation emits only what the validator accepts is required under either option; the prefix question needs a product decision. Flagged for human decision.
notes: Decision (operator, 2026-06-11): option (i) — add a skill: surface prefix to the spec plus a skill-directory inventory extractor. The generator-emits-only-validator-accepted-refs invariant is part of the same work.

title: Update shared spec for schema, copy, and flag evidence kinds
description:
`ss-evidence-epic` is open because its implementation children landed but the shared specification still describes only the older evidence model. Update `shared/spec.md` so it documents the three evidence kinds already implemented by the closed child issues:

- `ss-evidence-schema`: `schema:<table>.<column>` evidence and `schema-evidence-missing`
- `ss-evidence-copy`: `copy:<locale-file>#<key>` evidence and `copy-evidence-missing`
- `ss-evidence-flag`: `flag:<identifier>` evidence and `flag-evidence-missing`

Current code facts:

- `shared/storystore_lib.py` parses `Evidence.Schema`, `Evidence.Flag` or `Evidence.Flags`, and `Evidence.Copy` into story fields.
- `shared/inventory.py` resolves schema, flag, and copy evidence refs and reports missing refs through `schema_missing`, `flag_missing`, and `copy_missing`.
- `shared/audit.py` emits deterministic findings for `schema-evidence-missing`, `flag-evidence-missing`, and `copy-evidence-missing`.
- `shared/coverage.py` includes schema, flag, and copy evidence in evidence counting and coverage output.
- `tests/test_storystore_audit.py` already covers missing and resolved schema, flag, and copy evidence.
- `shared/spec.md` currently documents `Evidence.Tests`, `Evidence.Surface`, and `Evidence.Docs`, and its deterministic audit findings table lists `surface-missing` and `test-evidence-missing` but not the three newer missing-evidence findings.

Out of scope:

- Runtime changes to `shared/storystore_lib.py`, `shared/inventory.py`, `shared/audit.py`, or `shared/coverage.py`.
- New evidence kinds beyond schema, copy, and flag.
- Changes to audit severity rules.
- Changes to existing surface kinds.
acceptance_criteria: shared/spec.md documents Evidence.Schema, Evidence.Copy, and Evidence.Flag alongside existing Evidence subsections, including supported reference formats; shared/spec.md documents spec-level resolver behavior for schema refs against migration definitions, copy refs against JSON locale-file keys, and flag refs against source/config flag definitions; deterministic audit findings table includes schema-evidence-missing, copy-evidence-missing, and flag-evidence-missing; spec says these findings do not change the existing severity model; spec says schema/copy/flag refs count as evidence for coverage/completeness alongside tests, surface refs, and docs; scope is limited to documentation in shared/spec.md with no runtime behavior changes.

title: Switch LLM test harness from Ollama to Zolem fixture provider
description:
# Switch LLM test harness from Ollama to Zolem fixture provider

## Motivation

The three LLM test files (`test_storystore_llm_authoring.py`, `test_storystore_llm_narrative_audit.py`, `test_storystore_llm_init_quality.py`) use `OllamaProvider` — a direct call to Ollama's `/api/generate`. Tests skip when Ollama is unavailable and fail non-deterministically when small models return malformed JSON (the root cause of closed issue ss-llm-flaky-fix). Using Zolem's fixture backend eliminates both problems: tests run against pre-defined responses and require no live model.

## What Zolem Is

Zolem (`~/project/zolem`, `github.com/ketang/zolem`) is a local mock LLM provider server. In fixture mode it returns static JSON responses without any model inference. It exposes provider-compatible listeners (Anthropic `/v1/messages`) on dynamically assigned loopback ports.

## Proposed Change

### 1. Add `ZolemProvider` to `tests/llm_provider.py`

```python
class ZolemProvider(LLMProvider):
    def __init__(self, admin_url: str = "http://0.0.0.0:18090", listener_base_url: str = ""):
        self._admin_url = admin_url
        self._base_url = listener_base_url  # set by session fixture after listener creation

    def name(self) -> str: return "zolem"

    def is_available(self) -> tuple[bool, str]:
        # GET {admin_url}/_zolem/profiles — success means admin is running
        try:
            r = requests.get(f"{self._admin_url}/_zolem/profiles", timeout=2)
            return (True, "") if r.ok else (False, f"admin returned {r.status_code}")
        except Exception as e:
            return (False, str(e))

    def generate(self, prompt: str, *, system: str = "") -> LLMResponse:
        # POST to Anthropic-format listener
        body = {"model": "zolem-fixture", "max_tokens": 1024,
                "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]}
        if system:
            body["system"] = system
        r = requests.post(f"{self._base_url}/v1/messages", json=body, timeout=10)
        r.raise_for_status()
        data = r.json()
        return LLMResponse(text=data["content"][0]["text"], model="zolem-fixture", provider="zolem", raw=data)

    def list_models(self) -> list[str]:
        return ["zolem-fixture"]
```

### 2. Session fixture in `tests/conftest.py` (new)

`conftest.py` currently only has `collect_ignore_glob`. Add a session-scoped `zolem_provider` fixture:

```python
@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def zolem_provider():
    admin = "http://0.0.0.0:18090"
    # 1. Create fixture profile
    requests.put(f"{admin}/_zolem/profiles/storystore-fixture",
                 json={"backend": "fixture"}, timeout=5).raise_for_status()
    # 2. Create listener; response body contains "addr" key with bound address
    r = requests.put(f"{admin}/_zolem/listeners/storystore",
                     json={"addr": "0.0.0.0:0", "provider": "anthropic",
                           "profile": "storystore-fixture"}, timeout=5)
    r.raise_for_status()
    base_url = f"http://{r.json()['addr']}"   # key is "addr" (lowercase)
    return ZolemProvider(admin_url=admin, listener_base_url=base_url)
```

Add `require_zolem(zolem_provider)` helper that calls `pytest.skip()` when `is_available()` returns False.

### 3. Fixture response files

`tests/fixtures/zolem/<scenario>/response.json` — one per test scenario. Each file contains an Anthropic-format response with `content[0].text` set to a valid story JSON blob.

**Minimum story JSON for `write_story.py --observed`** (only `title` and `slug` are required; all other fields default in observed mode):

```json
{"title": "User views dashboard metrics",
 "slug": "user-views-dashboard-metrics",
 "intent": "...", "story": "...", "expected_behavior": "...",
 "boundaries": "...", "auditable_claims": ["..."],
 "evidence": {"tests": [], "surface": [], "docs": []}}
```

The full Anthropic response wrapper:
```json
{"id":"msg_fixture","type":"message","role":"assistant",
 "content":[{"type":"text","text":"<story json above>"}],
 "model":"claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022","stop_reason":"end_turn",
 "usage":{"input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":50}}
```

### 4. Update three LLM test files

Replace `OllamaProvider` / `require_ollama()` with `ZolemProvider` / `require_zolem()`. Inject `zolem_provider` session fixture into LLM-backed test classes. Deterministic classes (`TestProviderAbstraction`, `TestJSONExtraction`, `TestPromptStructure`) are unchanged.

## Acceptance Checks

- `ZolemProvider` passes the existing `TestProviderAbstraction` tests (or they are updated to cover both providers)
- All LLM-backed tests pass deterministically when Zolem admin is running with the fixture profile
- All LLM-backed tests skip with an informative message when the Zolem admin at `0.0.0.0:18090` is unreachable
- No Ollama required for LLM tests to pass (`OllamaProvider` remains available but is not the default)
- `python3 -m pytest tests/ -q --ignore=tests/test_install_codex_plugin.py` — 0 failures with Zolem running; LLM tests skip cleanly without it
- `scripts/build-plugin -v` succeeds

## In Scope

- `tests/llm_provider.py` — add `ZolemProvider`
- `tests/conftest.py` — add `zolem_provider` session fixture and `require_zolem()` helper
- `tests/fixtures/zolem/` — fixture response files (one per LLM test scenario)
- `tests/test_storystore_llm_authoring.py`, `tests/test_storystore_llm_narrative_audit.py`, `tests/test_storystore_llm_init_quality.py` — switch to `ZolemProvider`

## Out of Scope

- Modifying `shared/*.py`
- Removing `OllamaProvider`
- Standing up or configuring the Zolem admin server (assumed to be running at `0.0.0.0:18090`)

## Current Code Facts

- `LLMProvider` ABC: `name()`, `is_available() -> (bool, str)`, `generate(prompt, *, system="") -> LLMResponse`, `list_models() -> list[str]`
- `LLMResponse` dataclass: `text: str`, `model: str`, `provider: str`, `raw: dict`
- Zolem listener-creation response JSON key for bound address: `"addr"` (lowercase, from `localListenerView.Addr` struct tag in `cmd/zolem/local_admin.go:51`)
- `write_story.py --observed` requires only `title` and `slug` in payload; other fields get observed-mode defaults
- `conftest.py` currently has only `collect_ignore_glob = ["fixtures/*"]` — no existing session fixtures

title: Harden JSON extraction in LLM authoring tests to fix flaky test_llm_index_regenerated
description:
# Harden JSON extraction in LLM authoring tests to fix flaky test_llm_index_regenerated

## Symptom

`tests/test_storystore_llm_authoring.py::TestLLMAuthoring::test_llm_index_regenerated` fails non-deterministically with:

```
json.decoder.JSONDecodeError: Expecting property name enclosed in double quotes: line 8 column 70 (char 638)
```

The failure occurs inside `_extract_json` when Ollama returns output that wraps the JSON in prose or truncates it before the closing brace.

## Reproduction

Run with Ollama active and a small model (e.g. llama3.2:1b or similar):

```bash
python3 -m pytest tests/test_storystore_llm_authoring.py::TestLLMAuthoring::test_llm_index_regenerated -v
```

Failure is non-deterministic — small or heavily loaded models reproduce it most reliably.

## Root Cause

`_author_story` (line ~232) calls `provider.generate()` once and passes the result directly to `_extract_json` (line ~170). `_extract_json` uses brace-depth scanning to extract the first balanced JSON object; when `json.loads()` fails on the candidate it raises `json.JSONDecodeError`, which propagates as a test **failure** rather than a **skip**. There is no retry and no catch at either callsite.

## Desired Behavior

- Retry the full `provider.generate()` call (re-prompting Ollama) up to **3 times** when `_extract_json` raises `ValueError` or `json.JSONDecodeError`.
- After 3 failed attempts, call `pytest.skip("Ollama returned unparseable JSON after 3 attempts")` rather than raising.
- No inter-retry delay is required; add one only if Ollama rate-limits during testing.

## Acceptance Checks

- `_author_story` retries `provider.generate()` up to 3 times on extraction failure before skipping
- After exhausting retries the test skips (not fails) with an informative message
- Deterministic tests in the same file (`TestProviderAbstraction`, `TestJSONExtraction`, `TestPromptStructure`) are unaffected
- Manual check: `python3 -m pytest tests/test_storystore_llm_authoring.py -v` passes or skips cleanly across 5 consecutive runs with a small Ollama model (this is a manual/local check, not a CI requirement)

## In Scope

- `tests/test_storystore_llm_authoring.py` — retry logic and skip-on-failure in `_author_story` (and optionally more robust extraction in `_extract_json`)

## Out of Scope

- Changes to `shared/*.py`
- Changes to other LLM test files (`test_storystore_llm_narrative_audit.py`, `test_storystore_llm_init_quality.py`) unless they import `_extract_json` from this file
- Fixing the model or Ollama configuration

## Current Code Facts

- `_extract_json`: brace-depth scanner starting ~line 170; raises `ValueError` if no `{` found or unbalanced; raises `json.JSONDecodeError` if `json.loads()` fails on the candidate
- `_author_story`: ~line 232; calls `provider.generate(prompt, system=SYSTEM_PROMPT)` once, asserts non-empty, then calls `_extract_json(response.text)` — no retry, no catch
- `tests/llm_provider.py` `require_ollama()` fixture is the model for calling `pytest.skip()` — reuse that pattern

title: Require stories to capture edge cases and failure modes
description:
Title: Require stories to capture edge cases and failure modes

Problem

Storystore stories currently require `Intent` and define optional sections for `Story`, `Expected Behavior`, `Boundaries`, `Auditable Claims`, `Evidence`, and `Drift Notes`. The current authoring guidance for `stories-init` and `stories-generate` asks agents to draft useful observed stories, but it does not explicitly tell them to identify edge cases or failure modes. As a result, generated stories can describe the happy path and broad boundaries while missing observable negative paths that future agents need when evaluating behavioral changes.

This matters most during `stories-init`, because fresh init seeds the first observed stories for a repository. If those initial stories omit important failure behavior, future `stories-impact-check`, audit, and update work has weaker intent context from the start. The same expectation should apply to stories in general, including stories written through `stories-generate --interview` and `stories-generate --observed`.

Relevant current artifacts

- `spec.md` and `shared/spec.md` define the story body sections and the Draft Story Evaluation rubric.
- `skills/stories-init/SKILL.md` defines the fresh-init observed story workflow.
- `skills/stories-generate/SKILL.md` defines observed and interview story authoring plus independent draft evaluation.
- Existing tracker items include `ss-authoring-skills` and `ss-llm-init-quality`, but neither explicitly requires edge case or failure-mode coverage.

Design sketch

Keep the story schema backward compatible. Do not add a new required frontmatter field or make existing stories invalid. Instead, make edge-case and failure-mode capture part of the prose contract and quality gates:

1. Extend the story body contract in `spec.md` and `shared/spec.md`.
   - Update `Expected Behavior` guidance so it covers both normal behavior and important observable failure behavior.
   - Update `Boundaries` guidance so it asks for edge cases, exclusions, and known non-promises rather than generic disclaimers.
   - Add explicit wording that a high-quality story should mention meaningful edge cases and failure modes when they are known or inferable from deterministic evidence.
   - Preserve the current minimum creation bar of frontmatter plus `Intent`; sparse drafts remain allowed, but active or high-quality stories should be evaluated against the richer expectation.

2. Update authoring skills.
   - `stories-init`: during Phase 2, when drafting observed stories from candidate surfaces, require agents to inspect deterministic evidence for non-happy-path behavior such as validation errors, missing inputs, empty states, permission/auth failures, unsupported formats, timeout or network failures, idempotency behavior, and fallback paths.
   - `stories-generate`: for both observed and interview modes, prompt for or infer edge cases and failure modes, placing them in `Expected Behavior` and/or `Boundaries` as appropriate.
   - Make clear that agents must not invent failure modes. If evidence or human input does not support a failure-mode claim, either omit it or record uncertainty as draft-only wording.

3. Update the independent Draft Story Evaluation rubric.
   - Add evaluator checks for whether the story covers meaningful edge cases or failure modes that are evident from the supplied evidence packet.
   - Add or reuse finding kinds for weak negative-path coverage. If changing the JSON finding enum is too invasive, reuse `boundary-weak` for omitted edge cases/failure modes and document that interpretation.
   - Ensure promotion guidance treats missing obvious failure-mode coverage as at least a minor finding, and as a major finding when the missing behavior is central to the user-facing workflow.

4. Update qualitative tests and fixtures.
   - Add or adjust LLM/fixture quality expectations so fresh-init stories are scored for edge-case/failure-mode coverage.
   - Include at least one fixture where deterministic evidence exposes a clear failure path, such as invalid CLI arguments, missing config, nonexistent file path, or empty input.
   - Assert that the resulting story captures the failure path without overclaiming unsupported behavior.

Acceptance checks

- `spec.md` and `shared/spec.md` describe edge-case and failure-mode expectations for high-quality stories without making existing minimal story creation invalid.
- `skills/stories-init/SKILL.md` explicitly instructs fresh-init authors to look for and describe meaningful edge cases and failure modes when evidence supports them.
- `skills/stories-generate/SKILL.md` applies the same expectation to interview and observed story generation.
- The Draft Story Evaluation rubric checks negative-path coverage and gives actionable findings when obvious edge cases or failure modes are missing.
- At least one qualitative or fixture-backed test path covers an observed story with a deterministic failure mode and verifies the story does not stay happy-path-only.
- Documentation makes clear that agents must not invent unsupported failure behavior.

In scope

- Story contract wording, authoring-skill workflow guidance, evaluation rubric, and tests needed to enforce the new expectation.
- Backward-compatible additions to prose guidance and quality scoring.

Out of scope

- Adding new required frontmatter fields.
- Making existing stories invalid solely because they lack edge cases or failure modes.
- Implementing semantic inference beyond the current evidence packet and LLM authoring/evaluation flow.
- Requiring every story to contain a separate dedicated section for failure modes.

Size signal

This is a medium task because it touches the shared spec, generated skill guidance, and qualitative evaluation expectations. It should remain one issue if handled as a coherent contract/rubric update; split only if the implementation uncovers a large testing harness change.

title: Design glossary-as-prevention skill (ADR)
description: When an agent introduces a new identifier (e.g. Account) for a concept the repo already names (e.g. Org), drift is expensive to detect later — both names accumulate behavior across many files, and unifying them becomes a large refactor. Catching the candidate name at the moment an agent is about to introduce it is much cheaper and more decisive. This issue scopes the DESIGN of such a skill — an ADR-style decision document committed to storystore. Implementation is a separate follow-up issue once the ADR lands. The skill is PREVENTION. A separate parallel track addresses DETECTION of already-existing same-name drift via the object-catalog lens of the planned concepts-cohere skill (see storystore plan 2026-05-20-storystore-plan-4-concepts.md). The two tracks are complementary.
acceptance_criteria: Land an ADR document at docs/adr/<NNNN>-glossary-prevention.md (or storystore's equivalent ADR location if one is established) that decides: (1) Glossary file format and canonical location — default schema (YAML or markdown table); fields per entry (name, one-line definition, optional representative type/symbol, optional aliases); canonical path in the host repo. (2) Similarity method for candidate-name matching — one default (embedding-based or string-distance heuristic) with rationale; required dependencies/runtime; failure modes; threshold for 'near match'. (3) Invocation mechanism — choose one: (a) hook trigger that fires before file edits introducing new top-level identifiers, (b) explicit skill the agent is instructed to consult, or (c) both with one as primary. State choice and why. (4) UX on a flagged match — block, warn, return structured output. Choose one default. (5) Definition of 'concept-laden areas' — which file/symbol patterns the check applies to (new top-level type declarations, certain function patterns). State language-specific rules or a language-agnostic heuristic. (6) The two resolution paths — when flagged, the agent's two valid responses (rename to existing canonical entry, or add a new glossary entry justifying the new concept). Each decision states the choice, the alternatives considered briefly, and the reasoning. ADR enumerates explicit follow-up work (implementation issue ID and any dependent work). Out of scope: implementation; detection of drift in already-written code (concepts-cohere work in plan 4); seeding a real glossary in storystore.

title: Add evidence kind: config / feature-flag identifier
description: Add a 'flag' evidence kind that resolves flag:<identifier> references against the host repo's flag system. Reuses resolver/audit/coverage plumbing from ss-evidence-schema. Last of the three because flag systems vary and a thin discovery design is part of the scope. Parent: ss-evidence-epic.
acceptance_criteria: New evidence kind 'flag' defined and parsed. Deterministic resolver: given flag:experimental_collab, find the identifier in the host repo's flag definitions and return file path + line. Fails cleanly when not found. New audit finding 'flag-evidence-missing'. Coverage output lists 'flag' as a kind. Demonstrator story. Tests. First-version flag-system scope: plain string constants in a designated flags module (file path configured per host repo; default internal/flags/flags.go for Go projects, config/flags.yaml for YAML-style projects). Implementer picks one with rationale. SaaS providers (LaunchDarkly, Split, Statsig) deferred to follow-up issues. Out of scope: flag value/rollout state; provider integrations; flag dependency graphs. Depends on ss-evidence-schema (for pattern); ss-lib-parser, ss-inventory, ss-audit, ss-coverage.

title: Add evidence kind: user-facing copy string
description: Add a 'copy' evidence kind that resolves copy:<locale-file>#<key> references against locale files in the host repo. Reuses the resolver/audit/coverage plumbing established by ss-evidence-schema. Parent: ss-evidence-epic.
acceptance_criteria: New evidence kind 'copy' defined and parsed. Deterministic resolver: given copy:en/messages.json#errors.permission_denied, locate the key in the file and return file path + line. Fails deterministically when the key does not exist. New audit finding 'copy-evidence-missing', emitted by the audit script. Coverage output lists 'copy' as a kind. Demonstrator story. Tests. First-version format scope: JSON locale files (object hierarchy with dotted keys). YAML support deferred unless trivial. Other formats (gettext .po/.mo, ICU MessageFormat, Fluent .ftl) deferred. Resolver reports copy-evidence-missing cleanly on unknown formats, does not crash. Out of scope: text content matching (story claims about copy, not equality of the string); multi-locale resolution; copy normalization; pluralization rules. Depends on ss-evidence-schema (for pattern); ss-lib-parser, ss-inventory, ss-audit, ss-coverage.

title: Add evidence kind: schema column / migration
description: Add a 'schema' evidence kind that resolves a reference of the form schema:<table>.<column> against the host repo's migrations. Establishes the resolver/audit/coverage shape that the copy-string and flag-identifier children reuse. First child to land; the demonstrator pattern for the other two. Parent: ss-evidence-epic.
acceptance_criteria: New evidence kind 'schema' defined in shared/inventory.py (or wherever evidence resolution lives once ss-inventory lands) and reflected in shared/storystore_lib.py story-evidence parsing. Deterministic resolver: given schema:users.email, locate the most recent migration that defines or last modifies that column; resolver returns file path and line range. Resolver fails deterministically when no such column exists. New audit finding 'schema-evidence-missing' (analog of surface-missing / test-evidence-missing, see spec.md line 252-253), emitted by skills/stories-audit/scripts/. Default severity (no change to severity model). Coverage output (skills/stories-coverage/scripts/) lists 'schema' alongside existing kinds. One demonstrator story exercises a schema: evidence ref end-to-end. Tests follow existing storystore_lib conventions. First-version migration scope: Goose migrations (go-pgx-goose stack); plain .sql with CREATE TABLE / ALTER TABLE. Other migration tools (Flyway, Alembic, Prisma, sqlx) deferred — resolver reports schema-evidence-missing cleanly on unknown formats, does not crash. Out of scope: ORM type generation; query-string analysis; multi-database; live-database introspection. Depends on ss-lib-parser, ss-inventory, ss-audit, ss-coverage.

title: Evidence model: cover schema columns, copy strings, and config/flag artifacts
description: Storystore evidence today covers source files, tests, and three surface kinds (cli-command, http-route, package-bin) plus docs (see spec.md lines 252-255 for current finding types; spec.md line 323 for default surface kinds). Stories that anchor to schema columns, locale copy strings, or feature-flag identifiers have no clean evidence type. This epic groups three child issues that each add one new evidence kind, sharing a common resolver/audit/coverage shape modelled on existing surface kinds.
acceptance_criteria: All three child issues completed and merged: ss-evidence-schema, ss-evidence-copy, ss-evidence-flag. spec.md updated to enumerate the three new evidence kinds and their resolver/audit/coverage behavior alongside existing kinds. No regression to existing kinds. No changes to the audit severity model. Out of scope: changes to existing surface kinds (cli-command, http-route, package-bin); changes to the audit's severity model; cross-evidence inference rules. Depends on foundation issues ss-lib-parser and ss-inventory (evidence-resolution code paths must exist).

title: Add qualitative acceptance tests for fresh-init observed stories
description: Add opt-in qualitative tests that judge whether fresh stories-init produces useful observed stories, not just valid markdown.
acceptance_criteria: Run the full stories-init fresh-init flow against fixture repos with known user-facing surfaces; use local Ollama as an opt-in evaluator to score generated top 5 stories; evaluate candidate quality, duplicate avoidance, distinct capability coverage, unsupported-surface avoidance, user-facing Intent, specific Story, checkable Expected Behavior, concrete Boundaries, evidence-mapped Auditable Claims, and real Evidence refs when expected; assert minimum rubric scores, not exact text; store evaluator prompt, rubric, model name, and raw scores as test artifacts; skip cleanly unless STORYSTORE_LLM_TESTS=1 and Ollama is available; include a sparse-candidate fixture to verify fewer good stories are preferred over padding.

title: Add performance and scale regression tests
description: Add performance and scale tests for audit, coverage, inventory, and impact check.
acceptance_criteria: Generate synthetic repos with hundreds of stories and source files; assert audit, coverage, and impact-check complete under documented thresholds or emit expected warnings; verify skip dirs are honored; run normal-size perf tests by default and larger scale tests behind an opt-in env var.

title: Add LLM narrative audit test harness
description: Add opt-in LLM-backed tests for narrative audit findings.
acceptance_criteria: Add opt-in tests for narrative/D-pass style audit findings; use local Ollama to evaluate ambiguous, contradicted, and unsupported story prose in fixture repos; assert finding kinds and story slugs, not exact wording; include prompt/version metadata in test output for reproducibility; skip cleanly without Ollama.

title: Add LLM-assisted story authoring test harness
description: Add opt-in LLM-backed tests for observed story authoring using local Ollama.
acceptance_criteria: Define a provider abstraction for LLM-backed tests, initially supporting local Ollama; add test mode that asks an LLM to author observed stories from deterministic candidates; validate resulting stories through write_story.py and stories-audit; store prompts and expected structural assertions, not exact prose; tests are opt-in via STORYSTORE_LLM_TESTS=1; skip cleanly when Ollama is unavailable.

title: Add generated plugin contract tests
description: Verify generated Claude and Codex payload contracts in detail.
acceptance_criteria: Verify Claude and Codex generated payloads contain all expected skills, scripts, references, manifests, and assets; verify generated skill files include platform overlays where applicable; verify no build-only files like packaging.json leak into plugin outputs; verify generated scripts are executable where required; run after scripts/build-plugin.

title: Add golden report tests for audit, coverage, and impact check
description: Add golden-output regression tests for reports and policy workflow outputs.
acceptance_criteria: Add golden Markdown/JSON outputs for representative audit, coverage, and impact-check runs; normalize timestamps, temp paths, and duration fields; tests fail on unintended report format or finding-shape changes; include golden update workflow cases for locked-section refusal and drift todo append.

title: Add smoke and end-to-end plugin verification
description: Add smoke/e2e tests for the full generated Claude and Codex plugin payloads.
acceptance_criteria: Add smoke/e2e tests that build Claude and Codex plugin payloads; verify generated manifests and skill files; run representative init, generate, audit, coverage, update refusal, and impact-check commands; document the full verification command set.

title: Add repository docs and install instructions
description: Make the repository usable as the canonical storystore plugin project, not just a collection of scripts.
acceptance_criteria: Add or update README.md; add INSTALL.md; document build, tests, generated files, plugin installation, and skill responsibilities; keep docs aligned with implemented commands.

title: Implement drift todo append helper
description: Implement append-only drift todo helper for code-side story mismatches.
acceptance_criteria: Add drift_todo.py; default path is docs/stories/drift-todo.md; append date-stamped Markdown sections; preserve append-only behavior; tests cover create and append cases.

title: Trim bd context injection: .beads/PRIME.md override + memories-only PreCompact + minimal setup block
description:
bd prime injects ~768 words (~1,220 tokens) of mostly static workflow boilerplate at every Claude Code SessionStart, and again at every PreCompact, in any repo with a beads workspace (the beads plugin auto-runs it; some repos also wire it explicitly in .claude/settings.json). bd ships the fix as a supported customization point (see `bd prime --help`): a `.beads/PRIME.md` file overrides the default output entirely; `bd prime --export` dumps the default for editing; `bd prime --memories-only` emits only persistent memories (~45 words).

Work:
1. `bd prime --export > .beads/PRIME.md`, then trim to ~150-200 words keeping only: the condensed session-close checklist (status/add/commit/push), the six core commands (ready, show, create, update --claim, close, dep add), the no-TodoWrite and `bd remember` rules, and a closing pointer ("run `bd prime --full` for the complete reference").
2. Check this repo's `.claude/settings.json` (and be aware of the user-global one) for explicit bd prime hook wiring; where a PreCompact hook runs `bd prime`, switch it to `bd prime --memories-only` — after compaction the summary carries workflow state, and memories are the only content genuinely at risk.
3. Verify before committing: does a PRIME.md override still append persistent memories to SessionStart output? (The help text says the override replaces output "entirely".) If memories are suppressed, either end PRIME.md with an instruction to run `bd memories`, or add `bd prime --memories-only` as a second SessionStart hook.
4. If this repo's AGENTS.md carries a `<!-- generated by bd setup codex -->` ... `<!-- END BEADS CODEX SETUP -->` block, shrink the content inside the markers to two lines: "This repo uses bd (Beads). Run `bd prime` before tracker work. This block is intentionally minimal — do not re-run `bd setup codex`." Only a manual `bd setup codex` re-run regenerates it; `bd doctor` does not rewrite AGENTS.md content.
5. Commit `.beads/PRIME.md` (and any settings/AGENTS.md changes).

Clarifications: step 0 — confirm `bd prime --help` in this repo mentions the PRIME.md override and the `--export`/`--full`/`--memories-only` flags (all verified present in bd as of 2026-06-12; if this repo's bd predates them, upgrade bd or skip with a note). User-global settings (~/.claude/settings.json) are OUT of scope — modify repo-level wiring only and report any user-global bd prime hooks you notice. The provenance reference (bento-jdg) lives in the bento repo's tracker, not this one.

Savings measured in the originating repo: ~1,000 tokens per session start and ~1,150 per compaction. Origin: 2026-06 bento marketplace review (bento-jdg carries the full analysis and the master copy of this workaround).

Acceptance: `bd prime` in this repo emits the trimmed content (word count <= ~220); PreCompact wiring (if explicitly present) uses --memories-only; memories still reach the session (verified per step 3); any setup block is <= ~40 words.
close_reason: Landed: added compact .beads/PRIME.md override (~196 words) trimming bd prime context injection; closing pointer recovers memories via bd memories since override shadows --memories-only

title: Make the agent-instruction-file pointer a recorded first-class stories-init step, not a skippable follow-up offer
description:
## Symptom / motivation (pickpackit case study)

The pickpackit repo (`/home/ketan/project/pickpackit`) has had
`docs/stories/` with 5 stories since 2026-05-20, but its `AGENTS.md` and
`CLAUDE.md` contain ZERO mentions of stories or `docs/stories` (verified:
`grep -i stories AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md` finds nothing). Because no agent
instruction file points at the stories, no agent session ever triggered
stories-impact-check or stories-audit organically. The consequence
compounded: a product-wide rename (commit 4b8fba8, "Closet" to "my stuff")
sed-corrupted a story into `web/src/pages/My stuff.test.tsx` and route
`/my stuff` (`docs/stories/my-stuff-management.md:41,47`), and fabricated
evidence sat undetected for 3 weeks — the stories were effectively
invisible infrastructure.

Verified current behavior: `skills/stories-init/SKILL.md` (the "Follow-Up"
section, line 155) only OFFERS the pointer after Phase 2, as a one-time,
approval-gated, never-re-offered suggestion to add this line to each
detected agent-instruction file:

> This repo uses intent stories under `docs/stories/`. Before making
> behavioral changes to user-facing functionality, run
> `stories-impact-check`.

A skippable offer at the tail of init is exactly the step that gets
dropped — and once dropped it is never re-offered by design, so the wiring
gap is permanent.

## Desired behavior / change

Promote the pointer from a "Follow-Up" offer to a first-class, recorded
init step:

- It remains user-approval-gated (init must not silently edit AGENTS.md),
  but it is a numbered step in the init workflow whose outcome is
  recorded — applied, or explicitly declined by the user — not an optional
  offer.
- A declined pointer is recorded somewhere durable (e.g. the repo tracker
  item that init already requires for follow-up work, or the init report),
  so a later session can see that the repo deliberately opted out rather
  than silently fell through.
- Init's final report states the pointer status per detected instruction
  file.

Related but distinct: ss-tiv (gating the impact-check hard trigger on
stories existing) and ss-wwr (mechanical impact-check trigger).

## Acceptance checks

- Running stories-init on a fixture repo with an AGENTS.md ends with
  either the pointer text present in AGENTS.md or an explicit recorded
  decline; no third silent state exists.
- The SKILL.md no longer frames the pointer as a never-re-offered optional
  follow-up; it is part of the numbered init flow.
- The init completion report names each detected agent-instruction file
  and the pointer outcome for it.

## In scope / out of scope

- In scope: stories-init SKILL.md workflow restructure; recording the
  decision; report wording.
- Out of scope: mechanical (hook-based) impact-check triggering — that is
  ss-wwr; retrofitting existing initialized repos like pickpackit.

title: Provide a mechanical stories-impact-check trigger (PreToolUse hook or installable check script) keyed on story evidence refs
description:
## Symptom / motivation (pickpackit case study)

stories-impact-check has a prose "hard trigger" ("before any behavioral
change to user-facing surfaces, run this skill"), but prose triggers only
fire if the agent's instruction files surface them and the agent obeys. In
the pickpackit repo (`/home/ketan/project/pickpackit`) the trigger never
fired once in 3 weeks. Concrete damage: the Closet-to-"my stuff" rename
(pickpackit commit 4b8fba8, "adopt \"my stuff\" as canonical name for
user's documented possessions") was executed as a broad sed-style rename
that corrupted a story instead of updating it deliberately —
`docs/stories/my-stuff-management.md` now cites
`web/src/pages/My stuff.test.tsx` (line 41), route `/my stuff` (line 47),
and contains broken grammar like "searching your stuff" in
`docs/stories/trip-packing-list-management.md:17`. An impact check before
that rename would have listed the affected story and its evidence refs.

## Desired behavior / change

Add a mechanical trigger that does not depend on prose compliance: warn
when an edit touches files matched by story evidence refs.

- Matching semantics (pinned): treat story evidence refs as path
  prefixes/globs against the edited file's repo-relative path.
- Deliverable fork — left to the implementer, with trade-offs stated in
  the implementation plan:
  - (a) A PreToolUse hook (Claude Code style): intercepts Write/Edit
    calls, checks the target path against the evidence index, emits a
    warning (non-blocking) naming the affected stories. Pros: fires
    automatically, no workflow change. Cons: harness-specific, needs
    payload parsing, must be fast, and must fail open.
  - (b) An installable check script (run by agents/CI/land gates): given a
    list of changed paths, prints affected stories. Pros:
    harness-agnostic, testable, composable with ss-jrm's landing-gate
    direction. Cons: still needs something to invoke it.
- Either way the storystore repo should ship it as an installable artifact
  with documented installation, not a snippet in prose.

Adjacent, not duplicate: ss-jrm covers a pre-merge strict-audit landing
gate; this issue is about PRE-EDIT impact warning. Cross-reference rather
than merge.

## Acceptance checks

- With a fixture story citing `web/src/pages/Closet.tsx` as evidence, an
  edit/rename touching that path produces a visible warning naming the
  story before or at the edit, via the chosen mechanism.
- Prefix/glob matching is tested: evidence ref `web/src/pages/` matches an
  edit to `web/src/pages/Closet.test.tsx`.
- The mechanism fails open (a broken index or script never blocks
  unrelated edits) and is documented in the plugin docs.

## Acceptance note on the fork

The implementer picks (a) or (b) but must record the trade-off rationale
in the issue/PR; "both" is acceptable as two stacked deliverables.

## In scope / out of scope

- In scope: the trigger mechanism, evidence-ref matching, installation
  docs.
- Out of scope: pre-merge strict audit gating (ss-jrm); making the prose
  trigger stronger in agent docs (ss-e48); fixing pickpackit's
  corrupted story.

title: Add evidence-resolution accuracy to the story completeness rubric (or block "Complete" with unresolved refs)
description:
## Symptom / motivation (pickpackit case study)

All 5 stories seeded in the pickpackit repo
(`/home/ketan/project/pickpackit/docs/stories/`, created 2026-05-20) scored
"Complete" under the current rubric. The rubric measures volume-shaped
signals — word counts, number of claims, number of evidence refs — so a
story can score Complete while its evidence is wrong. Concretely, the
Complete-rated set contained:

- A fabricated endpoint `GET /api/workspaces/{workspace_id}/bootstrap`
  (`docs/stories/dashboard-trip-overview.md:41`; the real endpoint is
  `/overview`).
- A fabricated `DELETE /trips/{trip_id}/packing-items/{packing_item_id}`
  (`docs/stories/trip-packing-list-management.md:35,46`; removal is
  actually `POST .../remove`).
- Claims contradicted by the codebase and sed-corrupted refs such as
  `web/src/pages/My stuff.test.tsx` and route `/my stuff`
  (`docs/stories/my-stuff-management.md:41,47`).

"Complete" currently certifies that a story is long and well-populated, not
that it is true.

## Desired behavior / change

Make accuracy a first-class rubric input. Either:

- Add an evidence-resolution rate dimension (share of evidence refs that
  deterministically resolve) to the completeness rubric, or
- Keep the rubric as-is but block the "Complete" rating for any story with
  unresolved/failed evidence refs.

Dependency note (pinned): this needs the deterministic evidence-resolution
machinery from the companion issues filed in this batch — ss-c2v
(loud-failure contract; see also ss-jzs for the stub/audit gap) and
ss-xdk (generation-side verification). File it as dependent work, not
parallel work, so the rubric consumes resolution results rather than
reimplementing resolution.

## Acceptance checks

- A story containing a fabricated, mechanically-checkable evidence ref
  (file path or extractable route) cannot score "Complete"; the report
  names the unresolved refs.
- A fixture story modeled on the pickpackit case (high word/claim/ref
  counts, one fabricated endpoint) demonstrates the downgrade in tests.
- Stories whose only unresolved refs are explicitly `unverified` kinds
  (outside deterministic reach) have a defined, documented rating outcome —
  whichever policy is chosen, it is stated in the spec.

## In scope / out of scope

- In scope: rubric/scoring logic, report wording, spec update.
- Out of scope: building evidence resolution itself (ss-c2v,
  ss-xdk); generation-time blocking (ss-xdk); fixing pickpackit's
  stories.

title: Document a migration/import path from the bento intent-stories schema
description:
Storystore supersedes bento's intent-stories trio, but the schemas are incompatible (intent-stories: `id/protected_sections/last_reviewed`, authority charter|contract|story|observed|draft; storystore: `schema_version/slug/change_resistance/locked_sections`, authority accepted|observed). Repos with existing intent-stories corpora (and the bento marketplace itself, mid-migration) have no documented path. Work: document field mappings and an import mode (or a one-shot conversion script) — at minimum a section in spec.md describing how legacy stories map (e.g. protected_sections → locked_sections, authority collapsing rules), so the bento-side deprecation can proceed.

Acceptance: a documented (or scripted) conversion exists; bento's deprecation issue can reference it.

Scope note: designing the authority-mapping rules (charter|contract|story|draft to accepted|observed) IS the core work of this issue, not a precondition; document the chosen collapsing rules with rationale in spec.md.

title: Published bundle ships internal plans, full test suite, and .beads
description:
The installed cache (0.1.1) contains `2026-05-01-storystore-plan-{1,2,3}*.md` plus target-design (~60 KB), the full `tests/` tree (25+ files), and `.beads/`. Consumers need skills, shared/, scripts/, and spec.md. Shipping internal plan docs also confuses what the contract is (spec.md vs plans). Work: exclude internal docs, tests, and tracker state from the published artifact.

Acceptance: published bundle contains only runtime-needed files; install still passes its own verification.
close_reason: Landed in merge 4ff0a08 (--no-ff of ss-15i). Added .gitattributes with export-ignore for the 3 plan docs, target-design.md, tests/, and .beads/. GitHub honors export-ignore when generating the source tarball that both Claude's plugin cache (an archive extract, not a clone) and the Codex installer download, so consumers receive only runtime-needed files (skills/, shared/, scripts/, spec.md, README.md, INSTALL.md, plugin-version.json, .claude*/.codex-plugin payloads). build-plugin has no bundling step, so .gitattributes is the actual lever. Added tests/test_published_bundle.py to lock the contract via git archive. Codex installer validate_source requirements still present in the archive. Follow-up ss-9f7 filed for dangling plan-doc references in shipped SKILL.md/shared docstrings (out of this task's scope).

title: Give drift_todo a CLI; SKILL.md currently shows an inline import that only works from the plugin root
description:
`skills/stories-update/SKILL.md:59-67` instructs routing deferred drift via `from shared.drift_todo import append_drift_todo` — an inline Python import. `shared/drift_todo.py` has no __main__ or argparse (verified), and the snippet works only if cwd/PYTHONPATH is the plugin root, which the skill never establishes. Work: add a small CLI (`--slug --description --kind`) consistent with the other shared scripts and update the SKILL.md invocation (combined with the plugin-root preamble from the script-paths issue).

Acceptance: the documented drift-todo invocation runs from a consumer repo's cwd.

Sequencing: do after (or together with) the script-paths-claude-layout issue so the documented invocation uses the plugin-root preamble.
